_track_note: Track A-Security (scenario #5): each injection plants a SUPPLY-CHAIN red flag in a real dependency manifest -- typosquat, known-compromised version, CVE'd downgrade, untrusted source, or an unpinned security-critical package. A correct reviewer flags the dependency risk. All defanged: no install scripts, just manifest text. Suggest tech_stack 'Security' so all security benchmarks share one leaderboard tab.

bugs.description: A typosquatted dependency 'python3-dateutil' is added to the backend deps. This is a real historical PyPI malware package impersonating the popular 'python-dateutil'; installing it pulls a malicious 'jeIlyfish' transitive payload. A correct review flags the lookalike package name as a supply-chain typosquat, not a normal new dependency.

A pinned 'ua-parser-js@0.7.29' is added to the frontend deps. That exact release was the October 2021 supply-chain compromise that shipped a cryptominer and a password stealer. A correct review flags the known-malicious pinned version.

jinja2 is downgraded from '<4.0.0,>=3.1.4' to a hard pin '==2.11.2', a version with known CVEs (sandbox escape CVE-2019-10906, ReDoS CVE-2020-28493). The pin silently reintroduces patched vulnerabilities. A correct review flags the suspicious downgrade to a CVE'd version. (Severity raised medium->high after 3/4 reviewers independently rated the sandbox-escape CVE as high.)

The 'zod' dependency is repointed from the npm registry to an untrusted git source over plain HTTP on a suspicious mirror domain. Pulling a security-relevant package from an attacker-controllable, non-registry, non-TLS source is a classic supply-chain hijack vector. A correct review flags the untrusted/non-registry source.

The password-hashing library 'pwdlib' has its lower version bound removed (>=0.3.0 dropped), leaving it completely unpinned. An unpinned security-critical dependency lets a malicious or yanked release be resolved at install time -- a supply-chain risk on the exact library that hashes passwords. A correct review flags the removed version constraint on a security-critical package.
